title: Demand Planner and Buyer (Asst Manager / Senior / Executive)
description:
Roles & Responsibilities


About Us:

We are Singapore's premier wine, spirits, and beer supplier, prioritizing clients and employees. Our commitment to integrity, enjoyment, and teamwork drives us to excel. With a reputation for exceptional service, a diverse product range, and adaptability, we're dedicated to becoming the industry's top employer.



What We're Looking For:

The Demand Planner and Buyer will manage both demand planning and procurement activities within the organization. This role requires close collaboration with the management, sales, and logistics teams to ensure optimal product availability. The ideal candidate will utilize their analytical expertise to identify demand trends that drive business decisions. A background in FMCG and experience in demand planning and procurement processes will be highly advantageous.

By joining our team, you’ll have the chance to grow professionally and take on new challenges, helping you upgrade your expertise while contributing to the company’s success.



Responsibilities:


Demand Planning:

  • Develop and maintain accurate demand forecasting models to predict future product needs.
  • Analyze depletion reports to identify sales trends and generate product forecasts for procurement requirements.
  • Work closely with management, sales, and logistics teams to ensure the timely availability of products.
  • Continuously analyze data to identify trends and provide actionable recommendations for improvement.

Buyer:

  • Manage daily procurement and requisition activities, including handling shipments, and coordinating with vendors, warehouses, and freight forwarders.
  • Develop, implement, and regularly update standard operating procedures (SOPs) for procurement processes.
  • Ensure all procurement activities adhere to company policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements.
  • Oversee documentation for customs clearance, ensuring compliance with import/export laws and regulations.
  • Review vendor backlogs regularly and proactively expedite deliveries to meet deadlines.
  • Submit timely depletion reports to brand owners and relevant stakeholders.
  • Develop and maintain strong, positive relationships with suppliers, negotiating terms, pricing, and delivery schedules.
  • Evaluate supplier performance regularly, addressing any issues related to quality, delivery timelines, or service.
  • Implement cost-saving strategies without compromising product quality or delivery timelines.
  • Monitor inventory levels and lead times to prevent stockouts and overstocking, ensuring optimal stock availability.
  • Maintain high-quality standards for procured goods, conducting regular quality checks and inspections on received shipments.


Qualifications and Skills:

  • Ability to work autonomously, manage multiple tasks, and drive results with minimal supervision.
  • Diploma or Degree in Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
  • 2 to 3 years of experience in the FMCG industry, particularly in demand planning and procurement processes.
  • Understanding of international trade regulations, customs procedures, and import/export documentation.
  • Strong negotiation abilities, with excellent verbal and written communication skills.
  • Detail-oriented with a capacity to analyze data and make informed decisions.
  • Skilled in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) and SAP.
  • Knowledge of best practices and current trends in procurement and supply chain management.
